title: Tangy Molten French Sausage Stew
description: A tangy and savory stew featuring French sausages and bonito flakes, perfect for a brunch gathering. This dish is inspired by the cuisine of Brittany, France, and is Buddhist-temple-friendly.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large kazan cauldron over medium heat.
Add the sliced onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the sliced French sausage and cook until it is browned on all sides.
Add the sliced carrot and potato, and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the white wine, vegetable broth, tomato paste, and bonito flakes, and stir to combine.
Bring the stew to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender.
Season the stew with salt and pepper to taste, then serve hot and enjoy.
